items.title: Fuel costs drive COVID-like surge in demand for emergency relief

children.content: Rising petrol and diesel prices are driving Canberra families to seek emergency assistance and forcing small businesses to consider whether to pass on higher costs to customers.

items.title: Drug-fuelled driver who hit schoolboys sues Commonwealth over arrest

children.content: Tayler Hazell, who will be sentenced tomorrow over a hit-and-run that seriously injured two children, is suing the federal government, alleging police violently treated him during his arrest.

image.altText: Bodycam arret Tayler Hazell

items.title: 'Designed by youth': Service to offer free trauma support for Canberra teens

children.content: The new service, co-designed by young people, will provide therapy, peer support, and family services for teenagers experiencing trauma.

image.altText: A woman with long dark hair leans against a consultation room door smiling.

items.title: Canberra Liberals fail to reduce public transport fares as fuel prices soar

children.content: The Canberra Liberals introduced the motion for public transport fares to be temporarily cheaper to alleviate pressure on households while fuel prices rise, but it did not receive the support of the Assembly.

gallery.altText: Two signs on petrol pumps side by side say "no fuel".

thumbnail.altText: Two signs on petrol pumps side by side say "no fuel".

items.title: Man charged with murder and predatory driving over highway crash

children.content: A 40-year-old Canberra man has been charged with murder after a crash that killed a Bega Valley woman on the Monaro Highway in early March.

image.altText: generic sign of the monaro highway at nimmitabel

items.title: E-bike power crackdown and age limits introduced in NSW

children.content: The NSW government has recently announced staged reforms to improve safety for e-bike riders and pedestrians. This is what it'll mean for the community.

image.altText: Person waiting at lights on an fat tyre ebike

items.title: Canberrans encouraged to consider alternative transport amid fuel price hike

children.content: The ACT transport minister says there's "never been a better time" to consider purchasing an electric vehicle or using alternative transport modes as high petrol prices bite.

gallery.altText: A sign outside a petrol station with prices about $2 per litre for all types.

thumbnail.altText: A sign outside a petrol station with prices about $2 per litre for all types.

items.title: PM calls second emergency national cabinet meeting over fuel crisis

children.content: States and territory leaders will convene next week to further coordinate the national response to the fuel crisis as the fallout from the Iran war continues to escalate.

image.altText: Albanese wearing suit and tie looks serious

items.title: WA coast 'pummelled' as Cyclone Narelle could reach category five on approach

children.content: Authorities in Western Australia say they are preparing for weather not seen since 2021, when a deadly cyclone flattened buildings in a town unaccustomed to such extreme conditions.

articles.title: Track Cyclone Narelle live: Where is it forecast to cross WA coast?

children.content: Track the path of Tropical Cyclone Narelle as it heads towards the WA coast.

image.altText: An illustration shows a cyclone icon above the Kimberley coast in the north of Western Australia

articles.title: Seasons clash as polar blast joins summer cyclone on Australia's weather map

children.content: A clash of seasons is developing on Australia's weather map this week as a tropical cyclone tracks towards the WA west coast and a polar blast with thunderstorms forms over the south-east.

image.altText: A graphic of Australia with a cyclone icon on WA's west coast and a blue mass above Tasmania and far south-east.

items.title: ASX closes slightly down while NAB plans hundreds of job cuts — as it happened

children.content: The ASX 200 has closed slightly down as Iran's foreign minister says the country will not negotiate with the US on a ceasefire plan. Brent crude remains above $US102 a barrel.

image.altText: ASX

items.title: Meta and Google found liable in landmark social media addiction lawsuit

children.content: The case was brought on by a 20-year-old woman who accused the tech companies of causing harm by deliberately designing addictive platforms which worsened her mental health.

image.altText: Mark Zuckerberg looks surprised outside a cour hearing

items.title: 'Here I am': Sarah Mullally enthroned as first female Archbishop of Canterbury

children.content: Dame Sarah Mullally is enthroned as the Church of England's first female Archbishop of Canterbury, taking the helm as the spiritual leader of 85 million Anglicans worldwide in a historic ceremony blending tradition with global symbolism.

articles.title: Australian bishop surprised by appointment to senior Vatican role

children.content: Anthony Randazzo, the bishop of Broken Bay, which since 2019 covers Sydney's northern regions and the central coast, receives the personal title of archbishop and is named as Prefect of the Dicastery for Legislative Texts.

image.altText: two white men standing next to each other, they are in church robes with crosses around their necks

items.title: We've been here before as global crises tend to follow a predictable path

children.content: If the mass uncertainty sparked by the military escalation in Iran is making you feel anxious, you are not alone — but there is some calming news for us all.

items.title: Hard time for renters as landlords squeeze tight market

children.content: Australian renters continue to have a hard time finding a home, despite vacancy rates slowly improving from post COVID pandemic lows, a report finds.

items.title: The $88 million question facing Kyle Sandilands and radio network ARN

items.title: Ryswyk says reaction to coming out has been 'overwhelming'

items.title: Here's who you should see at comedy festivals across Australia

children.content: Comedy season is in full swing, with festivals across the country hosting the best acts from Australia and the world. We asked our favourites to share their recommendations.

items.title: What should you eat to keep your brain sharp as you get older?

children.content: The food choices we make over decades — not just in later life, but across adulthood — may quietly shape the health of our brains in ways that only become visible much later.

items.title: NFL confirms historic MCG game to be played on AFL semifinal weekend

children.content: The first-ever regular-season NFL game played in Australia has had its date confirmed, with the Melbourne Cricket Ground to host the historic match in the middle of the AFL finals series.

items.title: Bay the 'greatest risk and biggest asset' for beachside residents in Victoria

children.content: For residents of Victoria's Bellarine Peninsula like Vicki, who is facing the impacts of climate change-induced erosion and inundation, the water near their doors is both their biggest asset and greatest risk.

image.altText: Vicki Perrett stands in her driveway with the ocean in the background.

items.title: Brisbane capitalises on 'less sexy' business events to the tune of millions

children.content: Brisbane is punching above its weight as it attract more major business and scientific conferences that inject hundreds of millions of dollars into the city's economy each year, industry figures say.

image.altText: Brisbane Convention and Exhibition Centre

items.title: UK police arrest two men over arson attack on ambulances in London

children.content: British police arrest two men in connection with an arson attack on four ambulances belonging to a Jewish charity, which authorities are investigating as an antisemitic hate crime.

children.content: Former Brisbane Lions player Leigh Ryswyk has become the first openly gay AFL player, past or present.

items.title: Talk show host Stephen Colbert to co-write new Lord of the Rings movie

items.title: Bondi hero Ahmed Al Ahmed awarded key to city for bravery in terror attack

children.content: Father-of-two Ahmed Al Ahmed has been awarded a key to the city and a lifetime beach parking pass for his bravery in disarming one of the Bondi shooters during the December 14 terror attack.

image.altText: Mr Ahmed smiles as he stand with councillor Nemesh and Ms Beazley as they hold a plaque with a key on it.

items.title: Review calls for overhaul of WA's 'strictest' guardianship secrecy laws

children.content: People living under a guardianship arrangement in WA can't publicly speak up, but a review says this has to change.

children.content: Hong Kong police have arrested a bookstore owner and three shopkeepers for allegedly selling "seditious" publications.

items.title: Teachers' strike shuts Hobart and southern government schools

children.content: Industrial action by teachers seeking better conditions means government schools in southern Tasmania, including in the Hobart area, are shut today.
